pr.title: feat: clarify first session flow in landing hero
pr.body:
## Summary
- add a compact hero-adjacent "What happens next" strip to the landing page
- make the first session sequence more explicit by showing input, founder output, and next move in one place
- cover the new hero workflow copy in the landing page test

## What changed
- introduced a new `heroWorkflowSteps` data set in `src/app/page.tsx`
- rendered a new three-card workflow strip directly below the hero prompt form and above the existing hero insight/session-output panels
- used step framing that connects:
  - the raw founder prompt
  - the first founder brief artifact
  - the concrete next validation decision
- updated `src/app/__tests__/page.test.tsx` to assert the new hero workflow section renders with its key copy

## Why this change
The landing page already showed strong proof, session outputs, and a deeper first-session timeline further down the page, but the immediate hero area still left some ambiguity around what happens right after a founder submits a prompt.

This change tightens that first-run story in the highest-leverage spot on the page. It gives visitors a faster answer to:
- what do I start with?
- what will AI Cofounder give me back first?
- what concrete decision will I leave with?

That directly addresses a landing-page weakness where the workflow was credible but not fully legible at the point of conversion.

## Competitor/benchmark observations
This change was motivated by current public benchmark patterns observed during this run:

- Replit Agent uses an explicit step-based sequence to explain the first-run workflow: natural-language instruction, inspect/edit loop, then launch.
  - https://replit.com/agent
- Replit's product-market-fit page makes the loop legible as prototype -> launch -> feedback -> improve, which lowers ambiguity about what comes next.
  - https://replit.com/product-market-fit
- Lovable keeps the hero highly input/output-oriented, helping users understand what they type in and what concrete result they get back.
  - https://lovable.dev/
- Maze emphasizes output visibility and fast understanding of the resulting artifact, which is especially relevant for research/validation products.
  - https://maze.co/
  - https://maze.co/product/

The implementation here applies that pattern to AI Cofounder without copying competitor language: a small, hero-level workflow strip that clarifies the first session and the next founder move.

## Files changed
- `src/app/page.tsx`
- `src/app/__tests__/page.test.tsx`

## Validation
- `npm test -- src/app/__tests__/page.test.tsx`
  - passed (8 tests)
  - note: existing React `act(...)` warnings from `AuthButton` still appear in the test environment, but the suite passed
- `npm run build`
  - passed

## Risks / follow-ups
- this introduces some intentional conceptual overlap with the later "First session timeline" section, although the hero strip is shorter and focused on conversion-time clarity rather than the deeper walkthrough
- if future landing iterations want to reduce density, a follow-up could tighten or streamline the later timeline now that the hero explains the immediate flow earlier
pr.author: jianxux
